questions: how to edit video in Premiere Pro

answers: Adobe Premiere Pro is an industry-standard video editing software used by filmmakers, YouTubers, and professionals. It supports multi-track editing, color grading, audio mixing, and exports in all major formats.
Premiere Pro holo professional video editing software. YouTube video theke cinema — shob e edit kora jay. Timeline-based editor, color correction, audio editing shob ache.

To start with Premiere Pro, learn the workspace, timeline, razor tool, and transitions. Import your clips, arrange on timeline, add cuts and effects, then export. Keyboard shortcuts like C (razor) and V (selection) save tons of time.

Premiere Pro integrates seamlessly with After Effects, Audition, and other Adobe tools. It's the go-to choice for most professional video editors, though DaVinci Resolve offers a strong free alternative.
